title: dryopteris incisa
intro: Dryopteris incisa, also known as the cut-leaf or cut-leaved wood fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Dryopteris incisa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Dryopteris incisa can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
